strategies.name: Out-of-bounds extractelement index in cost modeling
strategies.target: An `extractelement` instruction with a constant index that is greater than or equal to the number of elements in the vector operand.
strategies.rationale: The fix prevents an out-of-bounds index from being returned by `getExtractIndex`. If an out-of-bounds index is returned, it is used to set a bit in an APInt (`DemandedElts.setBit(Idx)`), which causes an assertion failure if the index is greater than or equal to the bitwidth of the APInt (which is the number of elements in the vector). By providing an out-of-bounds index, we can trigger this assertion failure in the unpatched code.
strategies.expected_issue: The compiler will crash due to an assertion failure in APInt when trying to set a bit at an out-of-bounds index.

reason_thou:
Fix Understanding:
The patch modifies `getExtractIndex` in `SLPVectorizer.cpp`.
Before the patch, `getExtractIndex` for an `ExtractElementInst` simply returned the zero-extended value of the constant integer index.
After the patch, it checks if the index is out of bounds (i.e., `Idx >= VF`, where `VF` is the number of elements in the vector operand). If it is out of bounds, it returns `std::nullopt`.

Formal Semantic Modeling:
Before the fix: `getExtractIndex(EE)` returns `Idx` even if `Idx >= VF`.
After the fix: `getExtractIndex(EE)` returns `std::nullopt` if `Idx >= VF`.
The difference occurs when an `extractelement` instruction has an out-of-bounds index.
For example, `extractelement <4 x i32> %v, i32 4`.
Before fix: returns 4.
After fix: returns `std::nullopt`.

Assumptions and Preconditions Identified:
The code that calls `getExtractIndex` assumes that the returned index is within the bounds of the vector type.
For example, in `BoUpSLP::getEntryCost` (around line 15052):
`DemandedElts.setBit(*getExtractIndex(I));`
Here, `DemandedElts` is an `APInt` with a bitwidth equal to the number of elements in the vector. If `getExtractIndex` returns an out-of-bounds index, `setBit` will assert because the bit index is greater than or equal to the bitwidth.
Another place is around line 14443:
`DemandedElts.setBit(Idx);` where `Idx` comes from `getExtractIndex(EE)`.

Potential Cases to Break Assumptions:
An `extractelement` instruction with a constant index that is out of bounds. This is valid IR (it produces poison), but the SLP vectorizer's cost modeling logic was not handling it correctly, leading to an assertion failure in `APInt::setBit`.

Test Strategies:
We need to create a test case that triggers the SLP vectorizer to evaluate the cost of an `extractelement` instruction with an out-of-bounds index.
The test case should have a vector operation followed by an out-of-bounds `extractelement`, and this `extractelement` should be part of a scalar chain that the SLP vectorizer tries to vectorize or evaluate the cost for.
Actually, the bug report mentions "The cost modeling logic was attempting to set a bit in APInt for an out-of-bounds index, causing an assertion failure."
So a simple test case with an out-of-bounds `extractelement` that gets considered by SLP vectorizer will do.
test_traj:
{"found": false, "tool": "difftest", "action": "test", "args": "-S -passes=slp-vectorizer", "original_ir": "
define <4 x i32> @test(<4 x i32> %A) {
entry:
  %e0 = extractelement <4 x i32> %A, i64 0
  %e1 = extractelement <4 x i32> %A, i64 1
  %e2 = extractelement <4 x i32> %A, i64 2
  %e3 = extractelement <4 x i32> %A, i64 3
  %oob = extractelement <4 x i32> %A, i64 4
  %d0 = sdiv i32 %e0, %e0
  %d1 = sdiv i32 %oob, %e1
  %d2 = sdiv i32 %e2, %e3
  %d3 = sdiv i32 %oob, %e1
  %v0 = insertelement <4 x i32> poison, i32 %d0, i64 0
  %v1 = insertelement <4 x i32> %v0, i32 %d1, i64 1
  %v2 = insertelement <4 x i32> %v1, i32 %d2, i64 2
  %v3 = insertelement <4 x i32> %v2, i32 %d3, i64 3
  ret <4 x i32> %v3
}

define <4 x i32> @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call <4 x i32> @test(<4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 2, i32 3, i32 4>)
  ret <4 x i32> %r
}
", "transformed_ir": "

define <4 x i32> @test(<4 x i32> %A) {
entry:
  %0 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %A, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 poison, i32 poison, i32 2>
  %1 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %A, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 1, i32 3>
  %2 = sdiv <4 x i32> %0, %1
  %3 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %2, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 3, i32 2>
  ret <4 x i32> %3
}

define <4 x i32> @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call <4 x i32> @test(<4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 2, i32 3, i32 4>)
  ret <4 x i32> %r
}
